full_prompt: Design the primary pressure-shell module of a 4-crew Lunar Base Camp for a team space-design benchmark. Single cylindrical module with hemispherical endcaps; internal volume 50 m^3, outer diameter 4.0 m, cylindrical length 4.0 m, total length 8.0 m. Launch-mass cap per RFP baseline: 12,000 kg wet including shell + ECLSS + structure. Radiation areal density target (GCR+SPE): 15 g/cm^2 equivalent polyethylene or regolith sandbag layer atop Al shell. Shell material: Aluminum 2219-T87 (Young's modulus 73 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 352 MPa, ultimate 455 MPa, density 2840 kg/m^3), nominal wall 5 mm. Internal pressure MEOP = 70 kPa (hypobaric baseline), lunar external 0 kPa. Design factors per ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 + ANSI/AIAA S-080A metallic crewed: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4, proof = 1.5 x MEOP, burst = 2.0 x MEOP. Thermal cycle: -180 C (2-week lunar night) to +120 C (lunar day sun). Load cases: (LC1) MEOP 70 kPa internal; (LC2) proof 105 kPa; (LC3) burst 140 kPa must not produce ultimate failure; (LC4) launch quasi-static 9 g axial + 2 g lateral per SLS Block 1B; (LC5) thermal: external surface -180 C to +120 C, interior held at 22 C, with anisotropic radiation cladding on top surface; (LC6) lunar seismic 0.1 g horizontal. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, max von Mises <= 281 MPa (yield 352 / 1.25), (R2) under LC1, max stress <= 325 MPa (ultimate 455 / 1.4), (R3) under LC3 burst no ultimate failure; (R4) under LC4 launch loads, max stress <= 325 MPa; (R5) radiation areal density over crew bunk area >= 15 g/cm^2 (computed from regolith sandbag + shell); (R6) shell + structure mass + cladding <= 8000 kg (reserving 4000 kg for ECLSS); (R7) first cylindrical mode >= 20 Hz. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per pressure case, launch FEA, thermal gradient FEA, radiation shielding verification table, modal first 5 modes, and mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the primary pressure-shell module of a 4-crew Lunar Base Camp as a single cylindrical module with hemispherical endcaps, internal volume 50 m^3. Material class: Aluminum 2219-T87 shell, with an overlying radiation cladding (regolith sandbag or polyethylene) sized for >= 15 g/cm^2 GCR+SPE areal density over crew bunks. Qualify per ECSS-E-ST-32-02C Rev.2 + ANSI/AIAA S-080A metallic-crewed, with yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4, proof 1.5 x MEOP, burst 2.0 x MEOP.

Envelope: 4.0 m OD, 8.0 m total length; launch-mass cap 12,000 kg wet including shell + ECLSS.

Load cases.
- LC1 MEOP: 70 kPa internal (hypobaric baseline), 0 kPa external.
- LC2 proof: 105 kPa.
- LC3 burst: 140 kPa (no ultimate failure).
- LC4 launch quasi-static: 9 g axial + 2 g lateral per SLS Block 1B.
- LC5 thermal: outer skin -180 C (lunar night) to +120 C (day), interior 22 C, cladding on top surface.
- LC6 lunar seismic: 0.1 g horizontal.

prompt.title: Lunar Base Camp pressure module
prompt.brief: 4-crew Al-2219 cylindrical pressure module with regolith radiation shielding.
